costs: Establishment and ongoing operating cost of an independent statutory evidence body
Data-reporting compliance burden on tankerületi centers
Political cost if published data (e.g., on urban-school funding decline, KLIK deficits) proves embarrassing to current administration (F-live-political_feasibility-06)
content.equity_impact: By making segregation and disadvantage-concentration outcomes visible in a public registry, this proposal increases pressure for accountability on decisions with documented equity consequences (F-live-education_finance-06, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-05), but as an advisory framework it has no binding equity-protection power on its own and depends on other mechanisms (e.g., T1, T5) to convert visibility into enforceable safeguards.
expected_benefits: Provides a shared, verifiable evidence base currently absent, reducing reliance on contested or unverified cost-saving claims
Enables ex-post learning from past Hungarian closure/retention decisions rather than repeating politically-driven cycles without feedback (F-live-implementation_planning-02)
Supports better-informed application of any of T1-T5 by making true costs, thresholds, and outcomes visible
Establishes an explicit no-new-policy baseline for comparison, clarifying the marginal cost of inaction
content.goal: Establish a national, non-mandating framework of transparent minimum-viability thresholds, standardized cost-visibility rules, teacher-training standards, and a closure/outcome registry to inform local and central decisions across whichever structural model (T1-T5) is applied, while explicitly documenting the counterfactual of continuing current ad hoc practice.

implementation_steps.action: Establish an independent statutory body (or mandate an existing statistical/education-research institute) responsible for the viability registry, cost-visibility rules, and outcome tracking
Submit standardized cost, enrollment, transport, and segregation-indicator data annually to the registry for every village school
Publish annual public reports on realized (not projected) cost outcomes of past consolidations, closure/retention outcomes on segregation and achievement, and threshold-benchmark comparisons
Develop and publish national multigrade/rural-pedagogy training standards referenced by the registry
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Education / teacher training institutions

mechanisms: Hungary has experienced repeated historical cycles of rural school expansion and contraction without establishing a durable institutionalized feedback mechanism, meaning past sequences were driven by top-down political decisions rather than standing demographic-trigger rules (F-live-implementation_planning-02).

Whether school consolidation produces actual cost savings is contested internationally (NY studies found savings, Texas and multi-state studies found none), and Finnish municipalities cited cost-saving as rationale without rigorous follow-up verification, indicating a registry tracking realized (not projected) savings is needed before further consolidation decisions rely on cost claims (F-live-education_finance-02, F-live-finnish_reform-04).

Transportation cost offsets systematically reduce net fiscal savings from consolidation but are rarely fully counted in closure proposals, meaning cost-visibility rules must explicitly require their inclusion (F-live-education_finance-03).

Evidence on whether closure causes further population decline is genuinely mixed across contexts (Saxony/Sweden find no link, Finland and Denmark find a link), meaning any national framework should track Hungary-specific outcomes rather than assume international findings transfer directly (F-live-education_finance-04, F-live-demography-07).

A no-new-policy counterfactual (continuing current ad hoc administrative practice) is documented as producing 38% of settlements without any public educational institution and unresolved disputed transport-responsibility assignment, establishing the baseline against which a transparency framework's marginal value must be assessed (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06, F-live-legal_and_governance-05).
risks: An information-only framework may be ignored in practice given the historical pattern of top-down political decisions overriding technical evidence (F-live-implementation_planning-02, F-live-polish_reform-04)
Risk of registry capture or selective publication reducing its independence and credibility
Delay risk: establishing evidence infrastructure takes time during which the underlying demographic cliff (F-live-demography-05) continues
content.title: National evidence and transparency infrastructure: viability registry, cost-visibility rules, and no-new-policy baseline
